title: ‘Catabolic Capitalism’: How capitalism profits from collapse
content: From climate disasters to housing shortages, the system finds new ways to extract value from crisis. In this essay, author Craig Collins argues that modern capitalism is no longer built to expand prosperity, but to monetize collapse.
snippet: From climate disasters to housing shortages, the system finds new ways to extract value from crisis. In this essay, author Craig Collins argues that modern capitalism is no longer built to expand
creator: Craig Collins
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Mindset and worldviews shifts for a regenerative future
content: In this excerpt from the Seeds Series Volume 2, interviewees argue that deeper shifts in consciousness, economics and governance are needed to confront the polycrisis.
snippet: In this excerpt from the Seeds Series Volume 2, interviewees argue that deeper shifts in consciousness, economics and governance are needed to confront the polycrisis.
creator: Bill Baue
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: How to Think About the Future – Part 3: Uphill futures in a downhill world
content: Nate builds a framework for understanding the pathways that connect today’s choices to tomorrow’s realities. Drawing from biology, ecology, history, and systems thinking, he introduces a civilizational terrain of ridges and valleys that is constantly shifting as we are moving through it.

title: Making ‘Land Back’ a reality through an Indigenous community land trust
content: How the Wiyot people and local organizers are using an “honor tax” system to advance Indigenous self-determination and sovereignty.
snippet: How the Wiyot people and local organizers are using an “honor tax” system to advance Indigenous self-determination and sovereignty.
creator: Michelle Vassel

title: It’s the end of growth as we know it
content: Beyond living standards, economic growth has shaped how societies cope with shocks and distribute gains. As growth weakens, these less visible functions may prove just as consequential as its economic effects.
snippet: Beyond living standards, economic growth has shaped how societies cope with shocks and distribute gains. As growth weakens, these less visible functions may prove just as consequential as its economic
creator: Dr. Tim Morgan
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Iran is running out of water. The problem goes far beyond policy failure
content: From collapsing rivers to groundwater depletion, Iran’s water crisis is often framed as a failure of governance or infrastructure. But it reflects a broader reality: water stress is emerging wherever human demand pushes beyond ecological limits.
snippet: From collapsing rivers to groundwater depletion, Iran’s water crisis is often framed as a failure of governance or infrastructure. But it reflects a broader reality: water stress is emerging wherever
creator: Nastaran Rahnama
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Mombasa: Key outcomes from the Our Ocean Conference in Kenya
content: A major ocean conference has ended in Mombasa, Kenya, with just a handful of countries committing to high-level political declarations on banning deep-sea mining, protecting climate-resilient coral reefs and combatting illegal fishing.
snippet: A major ocean conference has ended in Mombasa, Kenya, with just a handful of countries committing to high-level political declarations on banning deep-sea mining, protecting climate-resilient coral
creator: Giuliana Viglione

title: Most of the world’s wealth belongs to all of us. It’s time to start collecting
content: As extreme inequality worsens and AI concentrates wealth into the hands of a small elite, insisting that the wealth generated by our collective inheritance be shared with all its rightful beneficiaries is not so much a political position as a moral imperative.

creator: Jeremy Lent

title: In defense of “nature”: why the word still matters in the age of AI
content: "Nature" is often cast as a word that separates humans from non-humans. But it can also mean something much larger: the universe, the course of things, the great event we live within. It's a word worth defending.

title: For the Love of Our Children: a poem by Esther Phillips
content: Environmental activist and poet Esther Phillips reflects on grief and anti-natalism in this poem, which explores the choice not to have children as an act of love in an age of collapse.
snippet: Environmental activist and poet Esther Phillips reflects on grief and anti-natalism in this poem, which explores the choice not to have children as an act of love in an age of collapse.

title: Inside an off-grid village of earthen domes – YouTube documentary review
content: Built from stacked earthbags and low-cost materials drawn largely from the surrounding landscape, the Mojave Center showcases an unconventional approach to homebuilding, constructed with the help of just a few volunteers. This documentary explores a compelling vision of off-grid life in the Arizona desert.
